required_edits: Correct the shared lexer’s significant-token state and regex-versus-division handling for all supported expression contexts, including value-producing string/template/regex tokens and declaration/block endings such as class bodies. Add direct classifier and closure fixtures proving the real division-adjacent dynamic import is included and load-shaped text in a regex after a class declaration creates no edge.

Extend b5 detection to every import/export alias or specifier-name position, including `export * as <ModuleExportName> from`, and require the stable `unsupported-module-lexical-profile` diagnostic for quoted namespace aliases. Add direct and closure-level regression units.

Make malformed or truncated dynamic-import tails/options fail closed rather than degrade to `literal:false`. At minimum, enforce balanced and correctly nested option delimiters and add stable-diagnostic tests for closed-call but truncated/mismatched option structures such as `import("./x.mjs", { )`.
Re-run the committed real-weaver closure-equality assertions and deterministic suite after the shared scanner corrections.
hard_stops: The shared AM-41 lexer/classifier is not correct over the supported profile. `lex()` leaves `prevSigWord` stale after string, template, and regex literals, and its brace heuristic classifies ordinary class bodies as expression/object braces. Consequently, valid code such as `function f(){ return "x" / import("\x2e\x2fdep.mjs") / 1; }` can have its real dynamic-import edge masked as a regex, while a regex after `class C {}` can expose load-shaped regex text as a false closure edge. This violates the required previous-significant-token decision across all expression-start contexts and makes D33 closure results unsound.

AM-41(b5) is not fully enforced. `parseModuleDecl()` rejects string-literal names only inside `{...}` lists, so the namespace re-export variant `export * as "ns" from "./x.mjs"` is accepted instead of throwing `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`. The frozen rule rejects every string-literal specifier/alias name in an import/export clause.

AM-41(b6) truncated-form enforcement is incomplete. For example, `import("./x.mjs", { )` has a closed call parenthesis but a truncated/unbalanced options object; `literalAt()` merely returns `null`, and the classifier reports a nonliteral site instead of failing with `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`. This permits a truncated accepted form to produce a successful classification/closure result.
rationale: The inventory constants, AM-40 package classification, normative inventory-id table, deeply frozen D32 mapping, D17 git/code-only implementation inventories, closure equality machinery, counted sources, containment helpers, git allowlist/weaver behavior, and code-weaver endpoint threading are substantially aligned with Task 4a. However, AM-41 correctness is part of the provenance boundary, not optional hardening. The concrete supported-profile false-negative/false-positive cases and the missed b5/b6 failures mean the shared classifier cannot be accepted AS-IS despite the deterministic gate passing.
